Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Targets fusion proteins to oil bodies, facilitating accumulation and purification of recombinant proteins
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of prior transgenic expression studies
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Oleosin fusion technology targets recombinant proteins to seed oil bodies. 2. This simplifies downstream purification and lowers processing cost. 3. It was used for proteins such as insulin, apolipoprotein AI, and human growth hormone in safflower.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful carrier for seed-based molecular farming and low-cost purification platforms
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core summarized gene/protein in the review because it is discussed as a specific molecular tool affecting accumulation/storage rather than a metabolic enzyme or regulator.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Fusion design may be product-dependent; limited to seed/oil-body production systems

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as an in-built protein-stabilizing agent protecting cytosol-targeted recombinant proteins
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of prior functional stabilization studies
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tomato cathepsin D inhibitor (SlCDI) can protect cytosol-targeted recombinant proteins in plants. 2. It is proposed as a stabilizing factor to improve accumulation. 3. Its value is mainly in molecular farming protein stability optimization.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a co-expression or fusion strategy to stabilize recombinant proteins in plant production systems
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient-metabolism gene; included only because the paper is about molecular farming rather than nutrient metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers β-1,2-linked xylose onto plant N-glycans
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key glycoengineering target; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes plant-specific xylose residues that are undesirable for human therapeutic glycoproteins; suppression/replacement improves humanized glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout, RNAi, and hybrid-enzyme engineering summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Plant β-1,2-xylosyltransferase adds plant-specific xylose to N-glycans. 2. This modification is undesirable for human therapeutics. 3. Knockout/suppression or use in hybrid engineering helps humanize plant glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for glycoengineering of plant bioreactors to remove immunogenic glycans
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: human β-1,4-galactosyltransferase I CST-domain hybrid context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper is not about nutrient metabolites; glycosylation of recombinant proteins is the focus.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering glycosylation may affect native plant glycoprotein processing

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose to terminal N-glycan residues in engineered plants
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key terminal determinant for humanized glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes human-like galactosylation and decreases undesirable xylose/fucose-containing glycans when used as hybrid enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knock-in expression of hybrid enzyme summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: improves therapeutic compatibility of plant-derived proteins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Human β-1,4-galactosyltransferase I was used in hybrid form for plant glycoengineering. 2. It caused high-level galactosylation of N-glycans. 3. It sharply reduced glycans with plant-type xylose and fucose residues.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable transgene for humanizing plant-made pharmaceuticals
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported as part of a domain-fusion hybrid enzyme containing CST domain
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May require precise sub-Golgi targeting for optimal glycoforms
